# ARCHITECTURE_V2 — Implementation Migration Plan

## Code Survival: ~60% Unchanged

### What Survives (No Changes)
- **Scoring engine** (`layer5/api/lib/scoring.ts`, `policy-engine.ts`) — 6-factor formula, 11-branch policy tree, Bayesian smoothing, LRU cache
- **MCTS simulation** (`layer5/api/lib/simulation/tier3-mcts.ts`, `tier1.ts`, `tier2.ts`, `tier-selector.ts`, `world-model.ts`)
- **10 of 13 recommendation engine files** — including task-performance, reason, cohort-cycle, semantic-action-cluster, outcome-weighting, data-freshness, rollout-flags, constants
- **All 133 SQL migrations** (`layer5/db/`) — star schema, materialized views, RPCs unchanged
- **All dashboard pages** (React + Vite + Tailwind, 15 pages, 12 hooks, 8 components)
- **Training pipeline** (`layer5/training/` — 7 files, LightGBM quantile regression)
- **Middleware** (`auth.ts`, `user-auth.ts`, `admin-auth.ts`, `validate-action.ts`, `rate-limit.ts`)
- **Type augmentation** (`types/hono.d.ts`)
- **CI/CD workflows** (4 files)
- **Ingestion pipeline** (`ingest-core.ts`, `outcome-score-inference.ts`, `verifier.ts`, `schema-inferrer.ts`, `context-embed.ts`)
- **Counterfactuals** (`ips-engine.ts`)
- **Orchestration** (`outcome-orchestrator.ts`, `decision-writer.ts`, `drift-detector.ts`, `predictive-drift.ts`, `sequence-tracker.ts`, `reward-backprop.ts`)
- **Queue** (`outcome-ingest-queue.ts`)
- **Adapters** (`langchain-adapter.ts`, `langgraph-adapter.ts`)
- **Supabase, sanitize, tenant-supabase, webhook-auth libs**
- **All 40+ test files**
- **Seed data, scripts, config.toml**

### What Gets Deleted
- **All 10 MCP tools** (`packages/mcp-server/src/tools/`): li-action, li-log, li-status, li-export, li-import, li-simulate, li-patterns, li-dashboard, li-configure, li-observe
- **param-resolver.ts** — no longer needed (no tool params to resolve)
- **task-intelligence.ts** — replaced by enrichment engine
- **episode-tracker.ts** — replaced by session-level episode tracking at gateway
- **12 of 14 REST routes become internal** (only MCP proxy endpoints exposed externally)

### What Gets Rewritten
- **`packages/mcp-server/src/index.ts`** → Gateway proxy server (handles `tools/list` + `tools/call` passthrough)
- **`packages/mcp-server/src/rest-client.ts`** → Upstream MCP connector (proxies to real tool servers)
- **`packages/mcp-server/src/config.ts`** → Supports multi-tool upstream config + mode config
- **`layer5/api/lib/outcome-orchestrator.ts`** → Adds decision_id generation + webhook callback handling

### What Gets Created (~22 new files)

**Gateway Core (7 files):**
1. `packages/mcp-server/src/gateway-proxy.ts` — Main proxy: intercept tools/list, enrich, forward tools/call
2. `packages/mcp-server/src/tool-enrichment.ts` — Natural language enrichment engine per mode
3. `packages/mcp-server/src/li-recommend-tool.ts` — Virtual li_recommend tool definition
4. `packages/mcp-server/src/mode-manager.ts` — Per-task-type mode resolution (Recommend/Assist/Auto)
5. `packages/mcp-server/src/decision-tracker.ts` — decision_id generation + episode tracking
6. `packages/mcp-server/src/upstream-registry.ts` — Register/health-check upstream MCP servers
7. `packages/mcp-server/src/fail-open.ts` — 3-layer failure protection implementation

**Enrichment Engine (3 files):**
8. `packages/mcp-server/src/enrichment/format-recommend.ts` — Recommend mode formatting
9. `packages/mcp-server/src/enrichment/format-assist.ts` — Assist mode formatting with warnings
10. `packages/mcp-server/src/enrichment/score-fetcher.ts` — Fetches scores from Hot/Warm layer for enrichment

**LLM Coach (2 files):**
11. `layer5/api/lib/llm-coach.ts` — gpt-4o-mini within-session coaching on failures
12. `layer5/api/lib/coach-session-tracker.ts` — Caps 3/session, retires as data accumulates

**Business Layer (2 files):**
13. `layer5/api/routes/business-webhook.ts` — External webhook callback ingestion endpoint
14. `layer5/api/lib/webhook-verifier.ts` — Webhook signature verification + secret management

**Safety & Operations (5 files):**
15. `layer5/api/lib/shadow-mode.ts` — Dry run: observe + compare without injection
16. `layer5/api/lib/environment-isolation.ts` — Staging/production outcome separation
17. `layer5/api/lib/model-versioning.ts` — Version + rollback probability models
18. `layer5/api/routes/model-history.ts` — Dashboard API for version diff, rollback, pin
19. `layer5/api/lib/confidence-thresholds.ts` — Per-agent per-task threshold enforcement

**New MCP Resources (3 files):**
20. `packages/mcp-server/src/resources/dashboard.ts` — Dashboard URL resource
21. `packages/mcp-server/src/resources/docs.ts` — Documentation resource
22. `packages/mcp-server/src/prompts/onboarding.ts` — Onboarding prompt

## Risks & Mitigations (Zero Users, Small LLM)

### Risk 1: Breaking existing MCP tools → ELIMINATED (zero users)
**Why:** No production agents depend on current MCP tools. Safe to delete all 10.

### Risk 2: LLM coaching cost at scale → LOW (small LLM design)
**Why:** gpt-4o-mini costs ~$0.15/1M input tokens. Coaching only fires on failures (~30% of actions). Capped at 3/session. Retires automatically as data accumulates. Even at 10K actions/day: ~$0.50/day max. Acceptable.

### Risk 3: Gateway latency overhead → LOW (simple proxy)
**Why:** LI is a thin proxy. tools/list interception adds 5-15ms (score lookup from Redis/memory). tools/call passthrough adds <2ms. Total overhead <20ms. Agent timeouts unaffected. Fail-open if slow.

### Risk 4: Natural language enrichment confuses some LLMs → ELIMINATED (dual channel)
**Why:** Natural language woven into descriptions (not bracketed metadata) — reads like any sentence. Virtual li_recommend tool as safety net. If agent ignores enriched descriptions, it can still explicitly call li_recommend. No single failure point.

### Risk 5: Auto mode reroutes wrongly → LOW (3-layer fallback + reversibility gate)
**Why:** Auto mode only on reversible actions. 3-layer fallback (2nd best → 3rd best → structured exception). Confidence thresholds prevent low-certainty rerouting. Graduated trust requires explicit developer opt-in per task type. Developer can rollback instantly.

### Risk 6: Migration complexity → LOW (clear boundaries)
**Why:** ~60% codebase untouched. Clear delete/create/modify boundaries. MCP server is greenfield within existing package. API routes mostly carry over. Database schema unchanged. Can implement incrementally: gateway proxy → enrichment → LLM coach → safety features.

## Unresolved Gaps (Operational/UX — Solve During/After Implementation)

1. **Multi-tool sequence scoring** — How LI scores read_file→analyze→push_fix as a sequence. Current architecture scores individual tools. Sequence scoring exists in codebase (mv_sequence_scores, sequence-tracker.ts) but enrichment currently only injects per-tool scores. Not a blocker — individual tool routing works first, sequence optimization follows.

2. **Latency budget specifics** — Architecture says "sub-millisecond Redis" and "50ms total" but no p95/p99 targets defined. Set during load testing.

3. **Rate limiting for high-volume agents** — 1000+ tool calls/minute. Rate-limit.ts exists (300 req/min, fail-open). Needs tuning for gateway proxy use case.

4. **Multi-tenant Redis isolation details** — Keys namespaced by Customer ID but implementation details (separate instances vs shared) depend on deployment infrastructure.

5. **Cost model for LI gateway** — Not architected. LI is open-source; users self-host. Gateway costs are their infra costs.

6. **A/B testing framework** — Shadow mode (dry run) exists as a safety feature, but formal A/B testing between LI-routed and non-routed not specified. Can be added post-launch.

7. **Dashboard pages for new V2 features** — Model history page, mode management UI, webhook configuration page, environment tag management, human-in-loop approval queue. These are frontend additions that don't block backend implementation.

8. **SDK sunset path** — TypeScript and Python SDKs exist but will be replaced by MCP gateway. No migration needed (zero users). Just stop publishing updates. Existing npm/pip packages remain for reference.

## Implementation Order (Recommended)

1. Gateway proxy core (tools/list interception + tools/call passthrough) — 7 new files
2. Tool enrichment engine (natural language formatting + score fetcher) — 3 new files
3. li_recommend virtual tool — 1 new file
4. Decision ID + episode tracking — 1 new file
5. LLM coach (within-session failure analysis) — 2 new files
6. Business webhook endpoint — 2 new files
7. Safety features (shadow mode, env isolation, model versioning, confidence thresholds) — 5 new files
8. Dashboard updates for V2 features — frontend additions
9. Delete old MCP tools — 10 file deletions
10. Test, load test, document

## Verification Checklist (Pre-Launch)

- [ ] tools/list enrichment works in Recommend mode (informational)
- [ ] tools/list enrichment works in Assist mode (directional + warnings)
- [ ] Auto mode silently reroutes to highest-probability action
- [ ] li_recommend returns ranked recommendations
- [ ] Decision ID generated and logged with every outcome
- [ ] Business webhook accepts and validates delayed outcomes
- [ ] Layer 2/3 outcomes correctly OVERWRITE Layer 1 scores
- [ ] LLM coach fires on failures, capped at 3/session
- [ ] Shadow mode observes without injecting
- [ ] Environment isolation (staging never pollutes production)
- [ ] Fail-open: agent executes directly if LI unreachable
- [ ] Local disk queue captures outcomes on async failure
- [ ] Model versioning: rollback and pin work from dashboard
- [ ] Drift detection auto-pauses on sudden success rate drops
- [ ] All 133 migrations apply cleanly (DB schema unchanged)
- [ ] All scoring/policy/MCTS tests still pass (60% codebase untouched)

4	metadata:

8	---
9	
10	# LayerInfinite — Complete Architecture & Codebase Knowledge
11	
12	## What LayerInfinite Is
13	
14	LayerInfinite is an open-source **decision intelligence layer for autonomous AI agents**. It sits between an agent's action selection and execution, providing:
15	
16	- **Scoring**: Bayesian-smoothed composite scores for every action, per task
17	- **Policy**: A deterministic decision tree telling the agent whether to exploit, explore, escalate, sandbox, or abstain
18	- **Simulation**: 3-tier what-if engine (Wilson CI → LightGBM → MCTS) predicting outcomes of action sequences
19	- **Trust**: Asymmetric exponential smoothing trust scores with coordinated failure detection
20	- **Recommendations**: Full recommendation engine with noise gates, stability gates, and LLM narratives
21	- **Observability**: Audit trails, discrepancy detection, drift monitoring, trend analysis
22	
23	## The 10-Layer Model
24	
25	| Layer | Name | What It Does |
26	|---|---|---|
27	| 1 | Structured Experience Memory | Star-schema DB (fact_outcomes + dim tables), 127 migrations, materialized views |
28	| 2 | Auth & Multi-Tenant | Dual auth (API key for agents, JWT for dashboard), customer_id scoping everywhere |
29	| 3 | Composite Scoring | 6-factor formula (success/confidence/trend/salience/recency/latency), Bayesian smoothing, LRU cache |
30	| 4 | Adaptive Policy | 11-branch pure-function decision tree, injectable randomFn, zero DB calls |
31	| 5 | Temporal Trending | OLS regression on last 20 outcomes, predictive drift alerts, trend labels |
32	| 6 | Trust & Suspension | Asymmetric exponential smoothing (failure ×0.9ⁿ, success ×1.03), 5-status lifecycle |
33	| 7 | Sequence Learning (IPS) | Inverse Propensity Scoring, softmax propensities, reward backpropagation (γ=0.85) |
34	| 8 | 3-Tier Simulation | Tier1 Wilson CI (<10ms) → Tier2 LightGBM (<100ms) → Tier3 MCTS with UCT (≤8s) |
35	| 9 | Training & World Model | LightGBM quantile regression (q10/q50/q90), 200 trees × 3 quantiles, 10-feature vector |
36	| 10 | Dashboard & Onboarding | React + Vite + Tailwind, Supabase Realtime, 12 hooks, 15 pages, onboarding flow |
37	
38	## Complete File Map
39	
40	### API Layer (`layer5/api/`) — 68 files
41	
42	**Entry**: `index.ts` (558 lines) — Hono app, middleware stack, route mounting, durable queue worker
43	
44	**Routes** (22 files):
45	- `log-outcome.ts` (1,603 lines) — Primary ingestion pipeline
46	- `import.ts` (1,423 lines) — Bulk file import, 5 formats, LLM schema inference
47	- `get-recommendations.ts` (1,149 lines) — Recommendation engine entry
48	- `get-scores.ts` (1,030 lines) — Ranked action scores with caching
49	- `discrepancy.ts` (823 lines) — Cross-event discrepancy detection
50	- `webhook.ts` (293 lines) — Stripe/SendGrid/generic webhooks
51	- `audit.ts` (272 lines) — Paginated audit trail
52	- `outcome-feedback.ts` (230 lines) — Delayed outcome feedback
53	- `pending-signals.ts` (190 lines) — Delayed signal registration
54	- `observe.ts` (168 lines) — Per-task outcome stats
55	- `simulate.ts` (141 lines) — 3-tier simulation
56	- `get-patterns.ts` (134 lines) — Sequence pattern retrieval
57	- `contracts.ts` (107 lines) — Signal contract CRUD
58	- `auth/me.ts` (19 lines) — Agent identity
59	- `auth/api-keys.ts` (166 lines) — API key management
60	- 7 admin routes (actions, drift, reinstate-agent, reinstate-sandbox, restore-trust, test-notification, trigger-training)
61	
62	**Lib Modules** (39 files):
63	- **Core**: `supabase.ts`, `sanitize.ts`, `tenant-supabase.ts`, `webhook-auth.ts`
64	- **Ingestion**: `ingest-core.ts` (1,118), `outcome-score-inference.ts` (426), `verifier.ts`, `schema-inferrer.ts` (663), `context-embed.ts` (512)
65	- **Scoring & Policy**: `scoring.ts` (706), `policy-engine.ts` (269)
66	- **Counterfactuals**: `ips-engine.ts` (219)
67	- **Orchestration**: `outcome-orchestrator.ts` (642), `decision-writer.ts`, `drift-detector.ts`, `predictive-drift.ts`, `sequence-tracker.ts`, `reward-backprop.ts`
68	- **Queue**: `outcome-ingest-queue.ts` (595)
69	- **Simulation**: `simulation/tier-selector.ts` (285), `tier1.ts`, `tier2.ts`, `tier3-mcts.ts` (309), `world-model.ts` (346), `types.ts`
70	- **Recommendation Engine** (13 files): `engine.ts` (1,162), `task-performance.ts` (933), `reason.ts` (822), `cohort-cycle.ts` (396), `llm-narrative.ts` (341), `scope-transition.ts` (147), `cohort-reliability.ts` (148), `semantic-action-cluster.ts` (218), `task-infer.ts` (244), `outcome-weighting.ts` (82), `data-freshness.ts` (33), `rollout-flags.ts` (127), `constants.ts` (9)
71	- **Adapters**: `adapters/langchain-adapter.ts` (238), `adapters/langgraph-adapter.ts` (306)
72	
73	**Middleware** (5 files):
74	- `auth.ts` (232) — X-API-Key/Bearer, SHA-256 hash, 60s cache
75	- `user-auth.ts` (168) — Supabase JWT for dashboard
76	- `admin-auth.ts` (38) — Admin role check
77	- `validate-action.ts` (509) — Action normalization + auto-registration
78	- `rate-limit.ts` (155) — 300 req/min, fail-open
79	
80	**Types**: `types/hono.d.ts` (24) — Hono context variable augmentation
81	
82	### Database (`layer5/db/`) — 133 SQL files
83	
84	- 127 migrations (001–131), star schema
85	- 22+ tables: `dim_agents`, `dim_customers`, `dim_actions`, `dim_contexts`, `dim_institutional_knowledge`, `dim_action_aliases`, `fact_outcomes` (append-only), `fact_outcome_counterfactuals`, `fact_decisions`, `fact_trust_snapshots`, `world_model_artifacts`, `recommendation_cohort_cycles`, `queue_outcome_ingress`, and more
86	- Materialized views: `mv_action_scores`, `mv_cluster_scores`, `mv_episode_patterns`, `mv_sequence_scores`, `mv_task_action_performance_180d`
87	- 6 Supabase Edge Functions: scoring-engine, trend-detector, cold-start-bootstrap, pruning-scheduler, notification-dispatcher
88	- 23 scripts in `scripts/` — deployment, health checks, migration governance
89	
90	### MCP Server (`packages/mcp-server/`) — 15 files
91	
92	- `src/index.ts` (198) — Server factory with 4-mode progression
93	- `src/episode-tracker.ts` (61) — In-memory Set, 30-min TTL
94	- `src/param-resolver.ts` — 3-layer param resolution (args → config → env)
95	- 10 tools: `li-action`, `li-log`, `li-status`, `li-export`, `li-import`, `li-simulate`, `li-patterns`, `li-dashboard`, `li-configure`, `li-observe`
96	- CLI, REST client, config, logger, prompts, resources
97	
98	### Dashboard (`layer5/` + `layer4/`) — 80+ files
99	
100	- React + Vite + Tailwind CSS + Supabase Realtime
101	- 12 custom hooks, 15 pages, 8 components, 6 settings pages
102	- Dual auth: Supabase JWT (dashboard) vs API key (backend)
103	
104	### Training Pipeline (`layer5/training/`) — 7 files
105	
106	- `train_world_model.py` — LightGBM quantile regression
107	- `features.py` — 10-feature extraction
108	- `validate_model.py` — Performance gates
109	- `export_model.py` — JSON serialization for TS evaluation
110	- `counterfactual_retraining.py` — Doubly Robust targets
111	- `DEPLOY.md`, `README.md`
112	
113	### Tests — 40+ files
114	
115	- `layer5/tests/` — Integration tests
116	- `layer5/api/tests/` — API route tests
117	- `layer5/training/tests/` — ML pipeline tests
118	- `packages/mcp-server/__tests__/` — MCP tool tests
119	
120	### CI/CD — 4 workflows
121	
122	- `ci.yml` — API tests only (missing training + MCP)
123	- `production-readiness-checks.yml`
124	- `publish-npm-sdk.yml`
125	- `publish-python-sdk.yml`
126	
127	## Key Data Flows
128	
129	### Outcome Ingestion Flow
130	```
131	Agent → POST /v1/log-outcome
132	  → Parse body → Idempotency check (SHA-256)
133	  → Verification (resolves verified_success)
134	  → Action resolution (normalize + auto-register)
135	  → Context resolution (embedding + cosine similarity, pgvector RPC)
136	  → Retry chain detection
137	  → Semantic clustering
138	  → Score inference (3-layer: hard signals → soft signals → latency)
139	  → Core insert (ingest-core.ts → fact_outcomes)
140	  → 8 side-effects via Promise.allSettled:
141	      1. Trust update (atomic RPC, exponential smoothing)
142	      2. Context drift check
143	      3. Silent failure detection
144	      4. Counterfactual computation (IPS)
145	      5. Sequence tracking
146	      6. Latency spike detection
147	      7. Cache invalidation
148	      8. Predictive drift check
149	```
150	
151	### Scoring Flow
152	```
153	Agent → GET /v1/get-scores
154	  → Check L1 cache (5s TTL, 500-entry LRU)
155	  → Fetch from mv_action_scores (or fact_outcomes fallback)
156	  → Apply 6-factor formula per action:
157	      composite = 0.40 × bayesian_success + 0.20 × confidence
158	                + 0.20 × trend + 0.10 × salience
159	                + 0.05 × recency + 0.05 × latency
160	  → IPS blending for low-sample actions (<20 episodes)
161	  → Cold-start guard (MIN_CONFIDENCE=0.15)
162	  → Cluster prior blending via mv_cluster_scores
163	  → Ambiguity detection (gap ≤ 0.05, both in 40-65% range)
164	  → Run policy engine (11-branch decision tree)
165	  → Cache results (5min TTL, 1000-entry LRU)
166	  → Return ranked scores + policy decision
167	```
168	
169	### Recommendation Flow
170	```
171	Agent → GET /v1/recommendations
172	  → Trust gate check
173	  → Scope selection (agent_scoped vs customer_blended)
174	  → Fetch task-action performance from mv_task_action_performance_180d
175	  → Shadow signals (simulation blend if enabled)
176	  → Noise assessment
177	  → Evidence threshold check
178	  → Ranking with Laplace smoothing
179	  → Confidence computation (harmonic mean of samples + lift)
180	  → Stability gates
181	  → Semantic convergence gate
182	  → Cohort reliability evaluation
183	  → LLM narrative generation (gpt-4o-mini, 1.5s timeout)
184	  → Build actionable output
185	```
186	
187	### Simulation Flow
188	```
189	Agent → POST /v1/simulate
190	  → Tier selector evaluates eligibility:
191	      Tier 3 requires ≥1000 episodes + loaded model + CI < 0.25
192	      Tier 2 requires ≥200 episodes + loaded model
193	      Tier 1 always available
194	  → Runs all eligible tiers in parallel
195	  → Tier 3: MCTS with UCT (C=√2), 500 sims, max depth 5, batch 10, 8s timeout
196	  → Tier 2: LightGBM ensemble, 600 tree evaluations
197	  → Tier 1: Wilson CI on mv_sequence_scores, fallback to cold_start_priors
198	  → Returns best result (highest tier that succeeded)
199	```
200	
201	## Key Algorithms
202	
203	### 6-Factor Scoring Formula
204	```
205	composite = 0.40 × bayesian_success_rate + 0.20 × confidence
206	          + 0.20 × trend_signal + 0.10 × salience_score
207	          + 0.05 × recency_bonus + 0.05 × latency_penalty
208	
209	Where:
210	  bayesian_success_rate = (successes + prior_strength × prior_mean) / (total + prior_strength)
211	  trend_signal ∈ {strongly_improving, improving, stable, declining, critical}
212	  salience_score = f(episode_position, outcome_magnitude)
213	  recency_bonus = f(days_since_last_outcome)
214	  latency_penalty = f(relative_latency_vs_baseline)
215	```
216	
217	### 11-Branch Policy Decision Tree
218	```
219	1. agent suspended           → ESCALATE
220	2. action new (no data)      → EXPLORE (force)
221	3. in sandbox                → SANDBOX (review)
222	4. cold_start                 → EXPLORE
223	5. low separation, ambiguous  → ABSTAIN
224	6. composite_score > exploit_threshold → EXPLOIT (epsilon-greedy)
225	7. composite_score > explore_threshold → EXPLORE
226	8. below_threshold            → ABSTAIN
227	9. epsilon-greedy roll       → EXPLORE (with probability epsilon)
228	10. default                   → EXPLOIT
229	11. (implicit) no valid path  → ABSTAIN
230	```
231	
232	### MCTS (Tier 3 Simulation)
233	```
234	For each simulation (500 total, batched in groups of 10):
235	  SELECT: traverse tree using UCT = win_rate + √2 × √(ln(parent_visits) / node_visits)
236	  EXPAND: add child node when leaf reached
237	  ROLLOUT: use Tier 2 world model for fast evaluation
238	  BACKPROPAGATE: update win rates up the tree
239	Best sequence = most-visited child path (robust policy)
240	```
241	
242	### Trust Score (Asymmetric Exponential Smoothing)
243	```
244	On success: trust = min(1.0, trust × 1.03)
245	On failure: trust = max(0.0, trust × 0.90)
246	Status: trusted(≥0.70) → probation(≥0.40) → sandbox(≥0.20) → suspended(<0.20)
247	New agents start at 0.50 (probation)
248	```
249	
250	### LightGBM World Model Features
251	```
252	1. action_encoded (label-encoded action ID)
253	2. episode_position (normalized 0-1)
254	3-5. prev_action_encoded × 3 (last 3 actions in sequence)
255	6. context_frequency (how often this context appears)
256	7-8. hour_sin, hour_cos (time-of-day cycle)
257	9-10. dow_sin, dow_cos (day-of-week cycle)
258	```
259	
260	## Caching Architecture
261	
262	| Cache | TTL | Size | In-flight Dedup |
263	|---|---|---|---|
264	| Auth | 60s | Unlimited Map | No |
265	| Action validation | 30min | Unlimited Map | No |
266	| Scoring | 5min | 1000-entry LRU | **No** (gap) |
267	| ML score baseline | 2min | 5000 entries | No |
268	| World model | 30min | Per customer | No |
269	| Narrative | 60s | Unlimited | Yes |
270	| Schema inferrer | Permanent | 100-entry LRU | Yes |
271	| Context embedding | 10min | Unlimited | Yes |
272	
273	## Graceful Degradation Patterns
274	- Simulation: Tier 3 → Tier 2 → Tier 1 fallback chain
275	- Scoring: MV → fact table → institutional priors → global fallback
276	- Task performance: MV → raw fact_outcomes fallback
277	- LLM narrative: gpt-4o-mini → null (caller falls back to static template)
278	- Cohort cycle: SQL RPC → in-memory Map fallback
279	- Rate limit: RPC → fail-open (⚠️ security issue)
280	- Decision writes: circuit breaker (3 failures → 60s open)
281	
282	## Multi-Tenant Isolation
283	- Every query scoped by `customer_id`
284	- `tenant-supabase.ts` enforces at type level (exhaustive union type)
285	- `ZERO_UUID_AGENT_ID` marks records excluded from blended scopes
286	- API keys scoped per customer via `dim_agents` join
287	
288	## Dual Auth Model
289	- **Agent API**: `X-API-Key` header or `Authorization: Bearer`, SHA-256 hash against `dim_agents.api_key_hash`, sets `agent_id` + `customer_id` on context
290	- **Dashboard User**: Supabase JWT via `Authorization: Bearer`, validates via `supabase.auth.getUser()`, resolves `customer_id` from `user_profiles`, sets `user_id` + `customer_id` on context
291	- **Admin**: Requires `adminAuthMiddleware` checking `dim_customers.config.role === 'customer_admin'`
292

---
name: layerinfinite-full-architecture
description: "Complete architectural understanding of LayerInfinite — what it is, the 10-layer model, full file map, data flows, key algorithms, database schema, API structure, and all subsystems. Compiled from 5-agent exhaustive exploration (2026-05-20)."
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: bad08b6b-9006-4a4b-acea-88f62b909625

# LayerInfinite — Complete Architecture & Codebase Knowledge

## What LayerInfinite Is

LayerInfinite is an open-source **decision intelligence layer for autonomous AI agents**. It sits between an agent's action selection and execution, providing:

- **Scoring**: Bayesian-smoothed composite scores for every action, per task
- **Policy**: A deterministic decision tree telling the agent whether to exploit, explore, escalate, sandbox, or abstain
- **Simulation**: 3-tier what-if engine (Wilson CI → LightGBM → MCTS) predicting outcomes of action sequences
- **Trust**: Asymmetric exponential smoothing trust scores with coordinated failure detection
- **Recommendations**: Full recommendation engine with noise gates, stability gates, and LLM narratives
- **Observability**: Audit trails, discrepancy detection, drift monitoring, trend analysis

## The 10-Layer Model

| Layer | Name | What It Does |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Structured Experience Memory | Star-schema DB (fact_outcomes + dim tables), 127 migrations, materialized views |
| 2 | Auth & Multi-Tenant | Dual auth (API key for agents, JWT for dashboard), customer_id scoping everywhere |
| 3 | Composite Scoring | 6-factor formula (success/confidence/trend/salience/recency/latency), Bayesian smoothing, LRU cache |
| 4 | Adaptive Policy | 11-branch pure-function decision tree, injectable randomFn, zero DB calls |
| 5 | Temporal Trending | OLS regression on last 20 outcomes, predictive drift alerts, trend labels |
| 6 | Trust & Suspension | Asymmetric exponential smoothing (failure ×0.9ⁿ, success ×1.03), 5-status lifecycle |
| 7 | Sequence Learning (IPS) | Inverse Propensity Scoring, softmax propensities, reward backpropagation (γ=0.85) |
| 8 | 3-Tier Simulation | Tier1 Wilson CI (<10ms) → Tier2 LightGBM (<100ms) → Tier3 MCTS with UCT (≤8s) |
| 9 | Training & World Model | LightGBM quantile regression (q10/q50/q90), 200 trees × 3 quantiles, 10-feature vector |
| 10 | Dashboard & Onboarding | React + Vite + Tailwind, Supabase Realtime, 12 hooks, 15 pages, onboarding flow |

## Complete File Map

### API Layer (`layer5/api/`) — 68 files

**Entry**: `index.ts` (558 lines) — Hono app, middleware stack, route mounting, durable queue worker

**Routes** (22 files):
- `log-outcome.ts` (1,603 lines) — Primary ingestion pipeline
- `import.ts` (1,423 lines) — Bulk file import, 5 formats, LLM schema inference
- `get-recommendations.ts` (1,149 lines) — Recommendation engine entry
- `get-scores.ts` (1,030 lines) — Ranked action scores with caching
- `discrepancy.ts` (823 lines) — Cross-event discrepancy detection
- `webhook.ts` (293 lines) — Stripe/SendGrid/generic webhooks
- `audit.ts` (272 lines) — Paginated audit trail
- `outcome-feedback.ts` (230 lines) — Delayed outcome feedback
- `pending-signals.ts` (190 lines) — Delayed signal registration
- `observe.ts` (168 lines) — Per-task outcome stats
- `simulate.ts` (141 lines) — 3-tier simulation
- `get-patterns.ts` (134 lines) — Sequence pattern retrieval
- `contracts.ts` (107 lines) — Signal contract CRUD
- `auth/me.ts` (19 lines) — Agent identity
- `auth/api-keys.ts` (166 lines) — API key management
- 7 admin routes (actions, drift, reinstate-agent, reinstate-sandbox, restore-trust, test-notification, trigger-training)

**Lib Modules** (39 files):
- **Core**: `supabase.ts`, `sanitize.ts`, `tenant-supabase.ts`, `webhook-auth.ts`
- **Ingestion**: `ingest-core.ts` (1,118), `outcome-score-inference.ts` (426), `verifier.ts`, `schema-inferrer.ts` (663), `context-embed.ts` (512)
- **Scoring & Policy**: `scoring.ts` (706), `policy-engine.ts` (269)
- **Counterfactuals**: `ips-engine.ts` (219)
- **Orchestration**: `outcome-orchestrator.ts` (642), `decision-writer.ts`, `drift-detector.ts`, `predictive-drift.ts`, `sequence-tracker.ts`, `reward-backprop.ts`
- **Queue**: `outcome-ingest-queue.ts` (595)
- **Simulation**: `simulation/tier-selector.ts` (285), `tier1.ts`, `tier2.ts`, `tier3-mcts.ts` (309), `world-model.ts` (346), `types.ts`
- **Recommendation Engine** (13 files): `engine.ts` (1,162), `task-performance.ts` (933), `reason.ts` (822), `cohort-cycle.ts` (396), `llm-narrative.ts` (341), `scope-transition.ts` (147), `cohort-reliability.ts` (148), `semantic-action-cluster.ts` (218), `task-infer.ts` (244), `outcome-weighting.ts` (82), `data-freshness.ts` (33), `rollout-flags.ts` (127), `constants.ts` (9)
- **Adapters**: `adapters/langchain-adapter.ts` (238), `adapters/langgraph-adapter.ts` (306)

**Middleware** (5 files):
- `auth.ts` (232) — X-API-Key/Bearer, SHA-256 hash, 60s cache
- `user-auth.ts` (168) — Supabase JWT for dashboard
- `admin-auth.ts` (38) — Admin role check
- `validate-action.ts` (509) — Action normalization + auto-registration
- `rate-limit.ts` (155) — 300 req/min, fail-open

**Types**: `types/hono.d.ts` (24) — Hono context variable augmentation

### Database (`layer5/db/`) — 133 SQL files

- 127 migrations (001–131), star schema
- 22+ tables: `dim_agents`, `dim_customers`, `dim_actions`, `dim_contexts`, `dim_institutional_knowledge`, `dim_action_aliases`, `fact_outcomes` (append-only), `fact_outcome_counterfactuals`, `fact_decisions`, `fact_trust_snapshots`, `world_model_artifacts`, `recommendation_cohort_cycles`, `queue_outcome_ingress`, and more
- Materialized views: `mv_action_scores`, `mv_cluster_scores`, `mv_episode_patterns`, `mv_sequence_scores`, `mv_task_action_performance_180d`
- 6 Supabase Edge Functions: scoring-engine, trend-detector, cold-start-bootstrap, pruning-scheduler, notification-dispatcher
- 23 scripts in `scripts/` — deployment, health checks, migration governance

### MCP Server (`packages/mcp-server/`) — 15 files

- `src/index.ts` (198) — Server factory with 4-mode progression
- `src/episode-tracker.ts` (61) — In-memory Set, 30-min TTL
- `src/param-resolver.ts` — 3-layer param resolution (args → config → env)
- 10 tools: `li-action`, `li-log`, `li-status`, `li-export`, `li-import`, `li-simulate`, `li-patterns`, `li-dashboard`, `li-configure`, `li-observe`
- CLI, REST client, config, logger, prompts, resources

### Dashboard (`layer5/` + `layer4/`) — 80+ files

- React + Vite + Tailwind CSS + Supabase Realtime
- 12 custom hooks, 15 pages, 8 components, 6 settings pages
- Dual auth: Supabase JWT (dashboard) vs API key (backend)

### Training Pipeline (`layer5/training/`) — 7 files

- `train_world_model.py` — LightGBM quantile regression
- `features.py` — 10-feature extraction
- `validate_model.py` — Performance gates
- `export_model.py` — JSON serialization for TS evaluation
- `counterfactual_retraining.py` — Doubly Robust targets
- `DEPLOY.md`, `README.md`

### Tests — 40+ files

- `layer5/tests/` — Integration tests
- `layer5/api/tests/` — API route tests
- `layer5/training/tests/` — ML pipeline tests
- `packages/mcp-server/__tests__/` — MCP tool tests

### CI/CD — 4 workflows

- `ci.yml` — API tests only (missing training + MCP)
- `production-readiness-checks.yml`
- `publish-npm-sdk.yml`
- `publish-python-sdk.yml`

## Key Data Flows

### Outcome Ingestion Flow
```
Agent → POST /v1/log-outcome
  → Parse body → Idempotency check (SHA-256)
  → Verification (resolves verified_success)
  → Action resolution (normalize + auto-register)
  → Context resolution (embedding + cosine similarity, pgvector RPC)
  → Retry chain detection
  → Semantic clustering
  → Score inference (3-layer: hard signals → soft signals → latency)
  → Core insert (ingest-core.ts → fact_outcomes)
  → 8 side-effects via Promise.allSettled:
      1. Trust update (atomic RPC, exponential smoothing)
      2. Context drift check
      3. Silent failure detection
      4. Counterfactual computation (IPS)
      5. Sequence tracking
      6. Latency spike detection
      7. Cache invalidation
      8. Predictive drift check
```

### Scoring Flow
```
Agent → GET /v1/get-scores
  → Check L1 cache (5s TTL, 500-entry LRU)
  → Fetch from mv_action_scores (or fact_outcomes fallback)
  → Apply 6-factor formula per action:
      composite = 0.40 × bayesian_success + 0.20 × confidence
                + 0.20 × trend + 0.10 × salience
                + 0.05 × recency + 0.05 × latency
  → IPS blending for low-sample actions (<20 episodes)
  → Cold-start guard (MIN_CONFIDENCE=0.15)
  → Cluster prior blending via mv_cluster_scores
  → Ambiguity detection (gap ≤ 0.05, both in 40-65% range)
  → Run policy engine (11-branch decision tree)
  → Cache results (5min TTL, 1000-entry LRU)
  → Return ranked scores + policy decision
```

### Recommendation Flow
```
Agent → GET /v1/recommendations
  → Trust gate check
  → Scope selection (agent_scoped vs customer_blended)
  → Fetch task-action performance from mv_task_action_performance_180d
  → Shadow signals (simulation blend if enabled)
  → Noise assessment
  → Evidence threshold check
  → Ranking with Laplace smoothing
  → Confidence computation (harmonic mean of samples + lift)
  → Stability gates
  → Semantic convergence gate
  → Cohort reliability evaluation
  → LLM narrative generation (gpt-4o-mini, 1.5s timeout)
  → Build actionable output
```

### Simulation Flow
```
Agent → POST /v1/simulate
  → Tier selector evaluates eligibility:
      Tier 3 requires ≥1000 episodes + loaded model + CI < 0.25
      Tier 2 requires ≥200 episodes + loaded model
      Tier 1 always available
  → Runs all eligible tiers in parallel
  → Tier 3: MCTS with UCT (C=√2), 500 sims, max depth 5, batch 10, 8s timeout
  → Tier 2: LightGBM ensemble, 600 tree evaluations
  → Tier 1: Wilson CI on mv_sequence_scores, fallback to cold_start_priors
  → Returns best result (highest tier that succeeded)
```

## Key Algorithms

### 6-Factor Scoring Formula
```
composite = 0.40 × bayesian_success_rate + 0.20 × confidence
          + 0.20 × trend_signal + 0.10 × salience_score
          + 0.05 × recency_bonus + 0.05 × latency_penalty

Where:
  bayesian_success_rate = (successes + prior_strength × prior_mean) / (total + prior_strength)
  trend_signal ∈ {strongly_improving, improving, stable, declining, critical}
  salience_score = f(episode_position, outcome_magnitude)
  recency_bonus = f(days_since_last_outcome)
  latency_penalty = f(relative_latency_vs_baseline)
```

### 11-Branch Policy Decision Tree
```
1. agent suspended           → ESCALATE
2. action new (no data)      → EXPLORE (force)
3. in sandbox                → SANDBOX (review)
4. cold_start                 → EXPLORE
5. low separation, ambiguous  → ABSTAIN
6. composite_score > exploit_threshold → EXPLOIT (epsilon-greedy)
7. composite_score > explore_threshold → EXPLORE
8. below_threshold            → ABSTAIN
9. epsilon-greedy roll       → EXPLORE (with probability epsilon)
10. default                   → EXPLOIT
11. (implicit) no valid path  → ABSTAIN
```

### MCTS (Tier 3 Simulation)
```
For each simulation (500 total, batched in groups of 10):
  SELECT: traverse tree using UCT = win_rate + √2 × √(ln(parent_visits) / node_visits)
  EXPAND: add child node when leaf reached
  ROLLOUT: use Tier 2 world model for fast evaluation
  BACKPROPAGATE: update win rates up the tree
Best sequence = most-visited child path (robust policy)
```

### Trust Score (Asymmetric Exponential Smoothing)
```
On success: trust = min(1.0, trust × 1.03)
On failure: trust = max(0.0, trust × 0.90)
Status: trusted(≥0.70) → probation(≥0.40) → sandbox(≥0.20) → suspended(<0.20)
New agents start at 0.50 (probation)
```

### LightGBM World Model Features
```
1. action_encoded (label-encoded action ID)
2. episode_position (normalized 0-1)
3-5. prev_action_encoded × 3 (last 3 actions in sequence)
6. context_frequency (how often this context appears)
7-8. hour_sin, hour_cos (time-of-day cycle)
9-10. dow_sin, dow_cos (day-of-week cycle)
```

## Caching Architecture

| Cache | TTL | Size | In-flight Dedup |
|---|---|---|---|
| Auth | 60s | Unlimited Map | No |
| Action validation | 30min | Unlimited Map | No |
| Scoring | 5min | 1000-entry LRU | **No** (gap) |
| ML score baseline | 2min | 5000 entries | No |
| World model | 30min | Per customer | No |
| Narrative | 60s | Unlimited | Yes |
| Schema inferrer | Permanent | 100-entry LRU | Yes |
| Context embedding | 10min | Unlimited | Yes |

## Graceful Degradation Patterns
- Simulation: Tier 3 → Tier 2 → Tier 1 fallback chain
- Scoring: MV → fact table → institutional priors → global fallback
- Task performance: MV → raw fact_outcomes fallback
- LLM narrative: gpt-4o-mini → null (caller falls back to static template)
- Cohort cycle: SQL RPC → in-memory Map fallback
- Rate limit: RPC → fail-open (⚠️ security issue)
- Decision writes: circuit breaker (3 failures → 60s open)

## Multi-Tenant Isolation
- Every query scoped by `customer_id`
- `tenant-supabase.ts` enforces at type level (exhaustive union type)
- `ZERO_UUID_AGENT_ID` marks records excluded from blended scopes
- API keys scoped per customer via `dim_agents` join

## Dual Auth Model
- **Agent API**: `X-API-Key` header or `Authorization: Bearer`, SHA-256 hash against `dim_agents.api_key_hash`, sets `agent_id` + `customer_id` on context
- **Dashboard User**: Supabase JWT via `Authorization: Bearer`, validates via `supabase.auth.getUser()`, resolves `customer_id` from `user_profiles`, sets `user_id` + `customer_id` on context
- **Admin**: Requires `adminAuthMiddleware` checking `dim_customers.config.role === 'customer_admin'`

I have now read all three architecture documents from memory:

- **`architecture_v2_complete.md`** — Full V2 gateway spec: tool discovery enrichment, 3 modes (Recommend/Assist/Auto), 3-layer DB with single atomic write, 3-layer outcome model, LLM coach, safety features
- **`architecture_v2_migration.md`** — ~60% of existing codebase survives, 22 new files to create, 10 MCP tools to delete, implementation order, risks/gaps
- **`project_full_architecture.md`** — Current 10-layer system: scoring, policy, simulation, trust, recommendations, 200+ files across `layer5/`, `packages/mcp-server/`, dashboard, training

## My Understanding

**LayerInfinite** is an MCP gateway that intercepts `tools/list` to inject historical success scores into tool descriptions, and proxies `tools/call` while logging outcomes async. It routes agents toward actions that historically worked, cutting token waste and wrong decisions.

**Current state**: A 10-layer scoring/recommendation API exists (`layer5/api/`) with 133 SQL migrations, dashboard, training pipeline, and an MCP server with 10 tools that will be replaced.

**V2 direction**: Replace the SDK-style MCP tools with a transparent gateway. Agent integration becomes 4 lines of JSON — no code changes. The existing scoring engine, DB, dashboard, and training pipeline largely survive.

**Key architectural insight validated**: Tool description enrichment at discovery time (`tools/list`) is the correct mechanism — it happens *before* the agent reasons, so no "intercepting the decision" protocol gap exists. Natural language weaving + virtual `li_recommend` tool provides dual-channel safety.

**Critical gaps identified**:
1. Multi-tool sequence scoring (existing in DB but not wired into enrichment yet)
2. Business/webhook layer exists in spec but needs implementation
3. Several dashboard pages for V2 features not yet built
4. The 30 issues from the 5-agent exploration remain to be addressed

What would you like to do now — begin the V2 migration, fix the 30 catalogued issues first, or something else?

148	  // ADMIN — Gated by LAYERINFINITE_ADMIN_KEY
149	  // ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
150	
151	  if (config.adminKey) {
152	    server.tool(
153	      LI_REGISTER_ACTION_NAME,
154	      LI_REGISTER_ACTION_DESCRIPTION,
155	      liRegisterActionSchema,
156	      createLiRegisterActionHandler(client, config.adminKey),
157	    );
158	  }
159	
160	  // ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
161	  // RESOURCE — Decision intelligence (always active)
162	  // ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
163	
164	  const taskReader = createTaskIntelligenceReader(client, config);
165	
166	  server.resource(
167	    'task-intelligence',
168	    new ResourceTemplate('layerinfinite://tasks/{task_name}', {
169	      list: undefined,
170	    }),
171	    taskReader,
172	  );
173	
174	  // ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
175	  // PROMPT — Mode-aware agent configuration (always active)
176	  // ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
177	
178	  server.prompt(
179	    AGENT_SETUP_PROMPT_NAME,
180	    AGENT_SETUP_PROMPT_DESCRIPTION,
181	    () => getAgentSetupPrompt(config),
182	  );
183	
184	  // ── Startup log ───────────────────────────────────────────
185	  const toolCount = 5 // foundation (4) + circuit breaker (1)
186	    + (mode ? 2 : 0) // intelligence
187	    + (mode === 'assist' || mode === 'auto' ? 2 : 0) // decision layer
188	    + (config.adminKey ? 1 : 0); // admin
189	
190	  logger.info('Server configured', {
191	    mode: mode ?? 'bootstrap',
192	    tools: toolCount,
193	    admin: config.adminKey ? 'enabled' : 'disabled',
194	    api: config.baseUrl,
195	  });
196	
197	  return server;
198	}

<summary>Agent "Planner implementation sequencing" completed</summary>
<result># LayerInfinite V2 Migration — Implementation Roadmap

## Executive Summary

This roadmap orchestrates two parallel migration vectors:
1. **Architecture V2**: Transforming the MCP server from a standalone tool suite into a gateway proxy with natural-language enrichment, decision tracking, and safety layers.
2. **Production Gap Closure**: Resolving 30 identified issues (4 critical, 7 high) across security, data integrity, framework compatibility, and intelligence layers.

Total estimated scope: **~60 files modified, ~35 files created, ~15 files deleted, 8 SQL migrations**. ~60% of the codebase (scoring, policy, MCTS, dashboard, migrations) remains untouched.

---

## Phase 1: Foundation & Security Hardening
**Duration**: 1–2 weeks  
**Milestone**: Production data is safe; critical vulnerabilities closed; schema boundaries hardened.

### Work
- **SDK Route Schema Hardening** (`layer5/api/routes/log-outcome.ts`): Add `.preprocess()` to `outcome_score` for percentage detection (85 → 0.85), `response_time_ms` string coercion, and structured field-level error responses.
- **Security Fixes** (from issues master):
  - Fix webhook auth bypass in `webhook.ts`: enforce mandatory verifier configuration before accepting payloads.
  - Fix RLS on `dim_contexts`, `dim_actions`, `dim_institutional_knowledge`: replace `USING (TRUE)` with `customer_id` scoping.
  - Rotate exposed secrets from `.env.local` and `layer5/e2e-verify.js`; add `.env.local` to `.gitignore`; scrub history with `git-filter-repo`.
- **Execute Backfill Migration 104**: Run `preview_fragmented_action_merges` → dry-run → audit review → live apply → `REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY mv_action_scores`.

### Deliverables
- New test: `layer5/api/tests/log-outcome-schema-coercion.test.ts` (4 cases: percentage coercion, string rejection, response_time_ms coercion, environment normalization).
- New script: `layer5/scripts/run-backfill-104.sql` (5-step maintenance window guide).
- New migration: `supabase/migrations/116_rls_dim_tables_fix.sql` (RLS policy corrections).

### Testing Gate
- [ ] All 440 existing API tests remain green.
- [ ] New schema coercion tests pass.
- [ ] Backfill 104 dry-run output reviewed and matches expected merge candidates.
- [ ] Secret-hygiene CI job passes with no tracked secrets.
- [ ] Manual verification: webhook auth rejected when no verifier configured.

### Risk Assessment
| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|------------|--------|------------|
| Backfill 104 corrupts action history | Low | Critical | Dry-run + 500k row limit + audit table + maintenance window with snapshot |
| Secret rotation breaks CI/dashboard | Medium | High | Rotate keys in staging first; keep old keys valid for 24h; test Supabase/Vercel connections before production cutover |
| RLS fix breaks dashboard reads | Medium | Medium | Test with dashboard integration tests; verify Supabase service role can still read for admin views |

### Rollback Plan
- **Backfill**: `action_fragmentation_backfill_details` table captures pre-merge state; rollback script restores original action names by joining to audit table.
- **RLS**: Migration is reversible; keep `USING (TRUE)` policy definitions in comment for emergency restore.
- **Secrets**: Keep old keys in password manager for 72h before full revocation.

---

## Phase 2: Gateway Proxy Core & MCP V2 Rewrite
**Duration**: 2 weeks  
**Milestone**: MCP server operates as a gateway proxy; old 10-tool suite sunsetted.

### Work
- **Delete old MCP tools**: Remove all 10 tools (`packages/mcp-server/src/tools/li-*.ts`), `param-resolver.ts`, `episode-tracker.ts`, `task-intelligence.ts`.
- **Rewrite MCP core**:
  - `packages/mcp-server/src/index.ts` → Gateway proxy server (tools/list interception + tools/call passthrough).
  - `packages/mcp-server/src/rest-client.ts` → Upstream MCP connector.
  - `packages/mcp-server/src/config.ts` → Multi-tool upstream config + mode config.
- **Create gateway core**:
  - `gateway-proxy.ts` — Main proxy with tools/list intercept and tools/call forward.
  - `upstream-registry.ts` — Register/health-check upstream MCP servers.
  - `fail-open.ts` — 3-layer failure protection (2nd best → 3rd best → structured exception).
  - `decision-tracker.ts` — `decision_id` generation + episode tracking at gateway level.
  - `mode-manager.ts` — Per-task-type mode resolution (Recommend/Assist/Auto).
  - `li-recommend-tool.ts` — Virtual `li_recommend` tool definition.

### Deliverables
- 7 new gateway files, 3 rewritten MCP core files, 15 deletions.
- Integration tests for gateway proxy passthrough.
- Health-check loop for upstream registry.

### Testing Gate
- [ ] `tools/list` enrichment works in Recommend mode.
- [ ] `tools/list` enrichment works in Assist mode.
- [ ] `tools/call` passthrough adds <20ms latency.
- [ ] Fail-open triggers correctly when upstream is unreachable.
- [ ] All existing MCP tests removed/replaced; new gateway tests pass.
- [ ] Load test: 1000 concurrent tools/list calls, p95 <50ms.

### Risk Assessment
| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|------------|--------|------------|
| Breaking existing MCP consumers | Very Low | Medium | Zero production users confirmed; SDKs sunset with no migration needed |
| Gateway latency spikes | Low | Medium | Fail-open design; benchmark before merge; Redis hot-path for score lookups |
| Upstream registry health check storm | Medium | Low | Exponential backoff on health checks; circuit breaker after 3 failures |

### Rollback Plan
- Gateway is completely new code path; rollback = revert to pre-V2 commit. MCP server package is isolated; no API routes depend on it. Keep old MCP package in a `packages/mcp-server-v1` backup branch for 1 week.

---

## Phase 3: Enrichment Engine & LLM Coach
**Duration**: 2 weeks  
**Milestone**: Gateway enriches tool descriptions with scores; within-session coaching available.

### Work
- **Enrichment engine** (3 new files):
  - `tool-enrichment.ts` — Natural-language enrichment engine per mode.
  - `format-recommend.ts`, `format-assist.ts` — Mode-specific formatting.
  - `score-fetcher.ts` — Fetches scores from Hot/Warm layer for enrichment.
- **LLM Coach** (2 new files):
  - `llm-coach.ts` — gpt-4o-mini within-session coaching on failures.
  - `coach-session-tracker.ts` — Caps at 3/session, retires as data accumulates.
- **Business webhooks** (2 new files):
  - `business-webhook.ts` — External webhook callback ingestion endpoint.
  - `webhook-verifier.ts` — Webhook signature verification + secret management.
- **Modify** `outcome-orchestrator.ts`: Add `decision_id` generation + webhook callback handling.

### Deliverables
- 7 new files created; 1 lib file modified.
- Coach fires only on failures (~30% of actions); capped cost ~$0.50/day at 10K actions.

### Testing Gate
- [ ] `li_recommend` virtual tool returns ranked recommendations.
- [ ] Decision ID generated and logged with every outcome.
- [ ] Business webhook accepts and validates delayed outcomes.
- [ ] LLM coach fires on failures, capped at 3/session.
- [ ] Shadow mode observes without injecting.
- [ ] Environment isolation (staging never pollutes production).

### Risk Assessment
| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|------------|--------|------------|
| LLM coaching cost at scale | Medium | Low | gpt-4o-mini (~$0.15/1M tokens); cap at 3/session; auto-retire as data accumulates |
| Natural language enrichment confuses LLMs | Low | Medium | Dual channel: woven into descriptions + virtual `li_recommend` safety net |
| Webhook verifier misconfiguration | Medium | High | Mandatory secret validation; fail closed on missing signature |

### Rollback Plan
- Enrichment engine is additive; disable via feature flag in `mode-manager.ts` (return un-enriched descriptions). LLM coach can be disabled by setting cap to 0. Webhook endpoint can be deregistered from routes.

---

## Phase 4: Framework Compatibility (LangChain / LangGraph / Cost Tracking)
**Duration**: 1.5–2 weeks  
**Milestone**: LangChain/LangGraph traces importable; token/resource cost tracked.

### Work
- **LangChain/LangGraph adapters**:
  - `api/lib/adapters/langchain-adapter.ts` — `flattenLangChainTrace()`: extracts `run_type: "tool"` runs, maps `name` → `action_name`, `error` → `success`, `token_usage` → metadata.
  - `api/lib/adapters/langgraph-adapter.ts` — `flattenLangGraphCheckpoint()`: state transition extraction.
- **Modify import route** (`layer5/api/routes/import.ts`): Add `format` query param (`auto`, `langchain`, `langgraph`, `csv`, `jsonl`); auto-detect LangChain by `runs[]` + `run_type`.
- **Token/Resource Cost Tracking**:
  - Migration 117: Add `resource_cost_units` and `resource_cost_type` to `fact_outcomes`; update `mv_action_scores` with `avg_resource_cost`.
  - Modify `log-outcome.ts`, `ingest-core.ts`, `import.ts` to accept and pass through cost fields.

### Deliverables
- 2 new adapter files, 4 modified route/lib files, 1 migration.
- 4 new tests: `api/tests/adapters/langchain-adapter.test.ts`, `langgraph-adapter.test.ts`, cost tracking tests.

### Testing Gate
- [ ] Import a real/synthetic LangSmith trace export; verify tool-level extraction.
- [ ] `format=auto` correctly detects LangChain vs JSONL vs CSV.
- [ ] Token usage from LangChain trace lands in `resource_cost_units`.
- [ ] `outcome_score: 85` coerced to `0.85` on import path.
- [ ] `mv_action_scores.avg_resource_cost` refreshes correctly.

### Risk Assessment
| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|------------|--------|------------|
| LangChain format variations | Medium | Medium | Adapter handles nested `child_runs[]` up to 3 levels; unknown formats fall back to raw JSONL parse |
| Cost column bloat on fact_outcomes | Low | Low | Nullable columns with partial index; only indexed where non-null |
| Import route regression | Medium | High | Existing 5-format parser untouched; adapters run as pre-processor |

### Rollback Plan
- Adapter logic is internal to import route; rollback = remove `format` query handling and let payload go through existing pipeline. DB columns are nullable; leaving them empty is harmless.

---

## Phase 5: Safety, Operations & Intelligence Layer
**Duration**: 2–2.5 weeks  
**Milestone**: Shadow mode, model versioning, predictive drift, and step scoring operational.

### Work
- **Safety & Operations** (5 new files):
  - `shadow-mode.ts` — Dry run: observe + compare without injection.
  - `environment-isolation.ts` — Staging/production outcome separation.
  - `model-versioning.ts` — Version + rollback probability models.
  - `model-history.ts` — Dashboard API for version diff, rollback, pin.
  - `confidence-thresholds.ts` — Per-agent per-task threshold enforcement.
- **Intelligence Layer**:
  - Migration 118: `mv_cluster_scores` materialized view; modify `scoring.ts` to blend cluster success rate as Bayesian prior for cold-start actions.
  - `predictive-drift.ts` — Simple linear regression on last 20 outcomes; emit `predicted_degradation` alert via `degradation_alert_events`.
  - Migration 119: `mv_step_performance` materialized view; modify `get-scores.ts` to return `step_performance` bottleneck signal when `episode_history` is provided.

### Deliverables
- 8 new files, 3 migrations, 3 modified lib/route files.

### Testing Gate
- [ ] Shadow mode observes without injecting.
- [ ] Model versioning: rollback and pin work from dashboard.
- [ ] Layer 2/3 outcomes correctly OVERWRITE Layer 1 scores.
- [ ] Predictive drift: 20 declining outcomes → alert emitted; stable → no alert; <20 outcomes → no attempt.
- [ ] Alert deduplication within 24h window.
- [ ] Step bottleneck detection: step success rate >20% below episode average flagged.
- [ ] All scoring/policy/MCTS tests still pass.

### Risk Assessment
| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|------------|--------|------------|
| Auto mode reroutes wrongly | Low | High | Only on reversible actions; 3-layer fallback; confidence thresholds; developer opt-in per task type |
| Predictive drift false positives | Medium | Medium | Linear regression is conservative; threshold at 0.3 success rate; dedup window prevents alarm fatigue |
| Model version rollback corrupts priors | Low | Critical | Rollback only affects future decisions; historical outcomes immutable; pin prevents accidental reversion |

### Rollback Plan
- Shadow mode: disable via config flag. Auto mode: revert to Assist mode globally. Model versions: pin previous version ID in `model-history.ts`. Predictive drift: disable cron job. Step scoring: omit `episode_history` from get-scores request.

---

## Phase 6: CI/CD Hardening & Test Gap Closure
**Duration**: 1 week  
**Milestone**: CI runs all test suites; training pipeline scoped; high-severity issues closed.

### Work
- **Fix CI** (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`):
  - Add `layer5/training/tests/` job (Python pytest).
  - Add `packages/mcp-server/__tests__/` job (gateway tests).
  - Add training pipeline typecheck/lint.
- **Fix high-severity issues**:
  - `verifier.ts`: Either implement downstream webhook call or remove the source type to prevent false assurance.
  - `rate-limit.ts`: Fail closed (429/503) on DB errors, not open.
  - `dim_actions`: Change uniqueness constraint from global to `(customer_id, action_name)`.
  - `train_world_model.py`: Add `customer_id` filter to all training data queries.
  - `config.toml`: Fix seed path from `./seed.sql` to `./seed/cold_start_priors.sql`.
  - `pruning scheduler`: Fix UPDATE mechanism or implement archival strategy.

### Deliverables
- 1 modified CI workflow, 3 modified lib files, 1 modified Python script, 1 modified config file.

### Testing Gate
- [ ] CI runs API + training + MCP gateway tests on every PR.
- [ ] Rate limit returns 429 when DB is unreachable.
- [ ] Training pipeline query plan shows `customer_id` filter.
- [ ] Two customers can register the same action name.
- [ ] `config.toml` seed path resolves correctly in fresh Supabase init.

### Risk Assessment
| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|------------|--------|------------|
| CI expansion reveals latent test failures | Medium | Medium | Run in parallel on feature branch first; fix failures before merging to master |
| Rate-limit fail-closed breaks health checks | Low | Medium | Exclude `/health` from rate limiting; return 503 (retryable) not 429 for DB errors |
| dim_actions constraint migration fails on duplicates | Medium | High | Pre-migration script identifies duplicates; manual resolution guide in migration comments |

### Rollback Plan
- CI changes: revert workflow file. Rate-limit: flip `failOpen` boolean back to true (documented in code). dim_actions: migration is reversible; remove composite index if rollback needed.

---

## Phase 7: Performance, Load Testing & Observability
**Duration**: 1 week  
**Milestone**: p95 latency targets met; caching stampede fixed; operational dashboards ready.

### Work
- **Performance fixes**:
  - Add in-flight dedup to scoring cache (`scoring.ts`) to prevent stampede on cold start.
  - Reduce `decision-writer.ts` flush interval from 5s to 1s (or implement write-through for critical decisions).
  - Increase MCP REST client timeout from 15s to 30s for simulation endpoints.
- **Load testing**:
  - Target: `tools/list` p95 <50ms, `tools/call` passthrough p95 <20ms overhead.
  - Target: Scoring endpoint handles 1000 concurrent agents without stampede.
  - Target: Import route handles 10MB LangChain trace without memory pressure.
- **Observability**:
  - Operational dashboard for V2: model history, mode management, webhook config, env tags, human-in-loop approval queue.

### Deliverables
- 3 modified lib files, load-test scripts, Grafana/dashboard updates.

### Testing Gate
- [ ] Load test: 1K concurrent `get-scores` requests, p95 <100ms.
- [ ] Cache stampede test: 500 simultaneous cold-start requests → 1 DB query.
- [ ] LangChain import: 10MB payload processes without OOM.
- [ ] Decision writer: 0 data loss on simulated crash.

### Risk Assessment
| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|------------|--------|------------|
| In-flight dedup adds memory pressure | Low | Low | Promise map auto-clears on resolution; 1000-entry LRU bounds concurrency |
| Load test uncovers deeper bottlenecks | Medium | Medium | Start with baseline measurement; fix only regressions >20% from current |

### Rollback Plan
- Scoring cache: remove promise map, revert to simple LRU. Decision writer: increase interval back to 5s. Load test changes are non-code (scripts only).

---

## Phase 8: Production Rollout & Validation
**Duration**: 1 week  
**Milestone**: V2 live for 100% traffic; all verification checklist items complete.

### Work
- **Gradual rollout**:
  - Deploy gateway to staging; run shadow mode against production traffic mirror.
  - Enable for 1% of agents → 10% → 50% → 100%.
  - Monitor `degradation_alert_events`, trust scores, decision latency.
- **Final verification checklist** (from architecture_v2_migration.md):
  - tools/list enrichment works in Recommend/Assist modes.
  - Auto mode silently reroutes.
  - Decision ID logged with every outcome.
  - Business webhook validates delayed outcomes.
  - Layer 2/3 outcomes overwrite Layer 1.
  - LLM coach fires, capped.
  - Shadow mode observes only.
  - Environment isolation enforced.
  - Fail-open works.
  - Local disk queue captures async failures.
  - Model versioning rollback/pin works.
  - Drift detection auto-pauses on drops.
  - All 133 migrations apply cleanly.
  - All scoring/policy/MCTS tests pass.

### Deliverables
- Rollout runbook, monitoring dashboards, post-mortem template.

### Testing Gate
- [ ] 48-hour shadow mode with zero discrepancies vs baseline.
- [ ] 24-hour canary at 10% traffic with no alert volume increase.
- [ ] Full traffic cutover with on-call engineer.
- [ ] Rollback drill completed in <5 minutes.

### Risk Assessment
| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|------------|--------|------------|
| Latency regression under real load | Medium | High | Fail-open ensures agents execute even if LI is slow; shadow mode catches this before injection |
| Trust score drift from new routing | Low | High | Trust scores update from outcomes, not routing; asymmetric smoothing is robust |

### Rollback Plan
- **Instant**: Disable gateway injection (agents see un-enriched tool list). Gateway becomes transparent passthrough.
- **5-minute**: Revert to pre-V2 Docker image / deployment.
- **15-minute**: Restore database from pre-cutover snapshot (only if data corruption detected; fact_outcomes is append-only so risk is minimal).

---

## Parallel Workstreams

| Workstream | Phases | Can Run Parallel With |
|------------|--------|----------------------|
| Security & Schema Hardening (Phase 1) | 1 | — |
| Gateway Core Rewrite (Phase 2) | 2 | Phase 1 (separate package) |
| Enrichment + Coach + Webhooks (Phase 3) | 3 | Phase 4 (different layers) |
| Framework Adapters + Cost Tracking (Phase 4) | 4 | Phase 3 |
| Safety + Intelligence Layer (Phase 5) | 5 | Phase 6 (CI fixes) |
| CI Hardening + Issue Closure (Phase 6) | 6 | Phase 5 |
| Performance + Load Testing (Phase 7) | 7 | — (requires Phases 2–5 complete) |
| Rollout (Phase 8) | 8 | — (requires all prior) |

**Maximum parallelization**: Phases 1+2 can start together. Phases 3+4 can run in parallel. Phases 5+6 can run in parallel. Total wall-clock time: **~8–10 weeks** with ideal parallelization.

---

## Critical Path

```
Phase 1 (Security/Schema) → Phase 2 (Gateway Core) → Phase 3 (Enrichment) → Phase 5 (Safety/Intelligence) → Phase 7 (Load Test) → Phase 8 (Rollout)
```

Phase 4 (Framework adapters) and Phase 6 (CI hardening) are off the critical path but must complete before Phase 8.

---

## Dependency Matrix

| Phase | Hard Depends On | Soft Depends On |
|-------|-----------------|-----------------|
| 1 | — | — |
| 2 | — | Phase 1 (security baseline) |
| 3 | Phase 2 | Phase 1 |
| 4 | Phase 1 | Phase 3 |
| 5 | Phase 2, Phase 3 | Phase 4 |
| 6 | — | Phase 1 |
| 7 | Phase 2, Phase 3, Phase 5 | Phase 6 |
| 8 | Phase 5, Phase 7 | Phase 4, Phase 6 |

---

## Estimated Scope Summary

| Phase | Files Modified | Files Created | Files Deleted | Migrations | Complexity |
|-------|---------------|---------------|---------------|------------|------------|
| 1 | 4 | 3 | 0 | 2 | Medium |
| 2 | 3 | 7 | 15 | 0 | High |
| 3 | 1 | 7 | 0 | 0 | High |
